name: America'S Credit Union, a
description_short: America's Credit Unions is a national trade association representing state and federally chartered credit unions, formed in 2024 through the merger of CUNA and NAFCU.
description_long: America's Credit Unions is a 501(c)(6) nonprofit trade association launched on January 1, 2024, following the merger of two major credit union industry groups: the Credit Union National Association (CUNA) and the National Association of Federally-Insured Credit Unions (NAFCU). The merger was announced on August 1, 2023, and approved by members of both organizations. The association is headquartered in Washington, D.C., at 99 M Street SE #300, and represents both state- and federally chartered credit unions across the United States. America's Credit Unions provides industry-wide trade association services to its member credit unions rather than direct consumer financial products or services. The organization focuses on advocacy, lobbying, regulatory engagement, professional development, and professional services management on behalf of the credit union industry. As a unified voice for credit unions, it represents member interests in policy discussions and regulatory matters at the federal level. What distinguishes America's Credit Unions is its role as a consolidated industry representative formed by merging two historically separate organizations. This consolidation was intended to streamline operations and create a stronger unified advocacy platform for credit unions. The organization is led by President and CEO Jim Nussle (with Scott Simpson announced to succeed him effective November 3, 2025). The merger included operational integration throughout 2024, including workforce reductions of approximately 25% at CUNA's Madison, Wisconsin headquarters. It is important to note that America's Credit Unions is NOT a credit union itself and does not offer consumer financial products, accounts, loans, or services. It is an industry association that advocates on behalf of credit unions. Consumers seeking credit union services must contact individual credit unions directly. The organization has actively engaged in policy debates, including filing lawsuit opposition to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's proposed overdraft fee restrictions in December 2024.
services: Trade association representation and advocacy for credit union industry
Federal lobbying and regulatory advocacy services
Professional development and training programs for credit union staff
Regulatory compliance guidance and industry standards
pros: Unified advocacy platform combining two major credit union industry organizations for stronger regulatory representation
Provides member credit unions with professional development and training resources
Offers regulatory advocacy and lobbying services to help credit unions navigate compliance requirements
Established network of state and federally chartered credit unions across the United States
Headquartered in Washington, D.C., positioned for direct engagement with federal policymakers
Provides professional services management support to member institutions
Operates as a 501(c)(6) nonprofit focused on industry benefit rather than profit
cons: Not a credit union or financial services provider—cannot offer consumer accounts, loans, or products
Recently underwent significant workforce reduction (25% of Wisconsin workforce in March 2024), potentially affecting service capacity during integration
Merger integration ongoing throughout 2024 with operational disruptions likely
Actively opposes consumer protection regulations (filed lawsuit against CFPB overdraft fee limits), raising questions about alignment with consumer interests
Membership organization—services available only to credit union members and institutions, not direct consumers
best_for: Credit union executives and managers seeking industry advocacy and regulatory guidance
State and federally chartered credit unions looking for professional development and trade association support
Credit union industry professionals needing access to lobbying and policy representation
Organizations seeking to understand credit union industry positions on regulatory matters
diagnosis: America's Credit Unions is appropriate ONLY for credit union institutions and industry professionals seeking trade association advocacy and regulatory representation, not for consumers seeking financial products or services. Consumers need to contact individual credit unions directly; this organization cannot provide consumer accounts, loans, or banking services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
